summary: Completed comprehensive testing of HIGH-severity Purchasing module fixes (H1 RBAC, H2 SoD, H3 PR reject modal, H4 UOM). Backend tests: 88.9% pass rate (16/18). Frontend tests: All H1 RBAC navigation tests passed for warehouse and sales users. H3 PR reject modal implementation verified via code review and backend API tests.

minor_issues.issue: Sales user cannot submit their own PR (403 Permission denied: purchase_requisition.update)
minor_issues.impact: Sales can create PRs but cannot submit them. This may be intentional design (requiring manager to submit), but should be clarified.
minor_issues.note: This might be expected behavior based on permission design. Sales has 'view, create' but not 'update' permission.

minor_issues.issue: PUT method returns 405 Method Not Allowed
minor_issues.impact: Supplier update endpoint uses different method (likely PATCH) or different route structure
minor_issues.note: Not related to HIGH-severity fixes. Minor API design issue.

sod_po_test_skipped.reason: PO created by admin went to 'pending' status instead of 'waiting_approval'
sod_po_test_skipped.note: PO value (50M IDR) may be below approval threshold, or approval rules configured differently. SoD logic is implemented correctly in code (line 322-326 in purchase_orders.py).
sod_po_test_skipped.recommendation: Verify approval_rules threshold settings or test with higher PO value

notes: H3 PR reject modal could not be fully tested via Playwright because no pending PRs were available in the UI during test run. However, modal implementation verified via code review (PurchaseRequisitionDetailPanel.jsx lines 180-199) and backend API tests confirm reject reason is properly saved.

passed_tests: H1 RBAC: Sales CANNOT GET /api/purchase-returns (403 - CORRECT)
H1 RBAC Frontend: Warehouse user sees Purchase Requisition and Retur Beli, but NOT PO
H1 RBAC Frontend: Sales user sees Purchase Requisition only, NOT Retur Beli or PO
H2 SoD PR: Manager creates high-value PR (pending_approval)
H2 SoD PR: Manager CANNOT approve own PR (400 with SoD message)
H2 SoD PR: Admin successfully approves manager's PR (200)
H4 UOM: Product created with base_unit='yard'
H4 UOM: Purchase return with unit='meter' auto-corrected to 'yard'
Backend authentication for all 4 roles (admin, manager, sales, warehouse)

clarifications_needed: Confirm if sales users are intentionally restricted from submitting their own PRs (they can create but not submit). If this is a bug, add 'update' permission to sales role for purchase_requisition module.
Verify approval_rules threshold for PO approval. Current test with 50M IDR PO did not trigger approval requirement. May need to test with higher value or check approval rules configuration.
recommendations: Consider adding a more user-friendly error message when sales users try to submit PRs if this restriction is intentional.
Document the permission model clearly: sales can create PRs but manager/admin must submit them.

h1_rbac_alignment.backend: All 4 API tests passed. Sales and warehouse can access purchase-requisitions. Warehouse can access purchase-returns. Sales correctly blocked (403) from purchase-returns.
h1_rbac_alignment.frontend: Navigation config correctly filters menu items by role. Warehouse sees PR + Retur Beli (no PO). Sales sees PR only (no Retur Beli, no PO).
h1_rbac_alignment.code_verification: navigationConfig.js lines 157-174 correctly defines role-based access. permissions_config.py correctly defines module permissions.

h2_segregation_of_duties.pr_sod: Manager created PR, attempted self-approval → 400 with 'Pemisahan tugas (SoD)' message. Admin approval succeeded. SoD logic working correctly.
h2_segregation_of_duties.po_sod: PO created by admin did not require approval (went to 'pending' instead of 'waiting_approval'), so SoD test was skipped. Code review confirms SoD logic is implemented (purchase_orders.py line 322-326).
h2_segregation_of_duties.code_verification: Both PR and PO routers check created_by_id == actor.id and block with appropriate error messages.

h3_pr_reject_modal.code_verification: PurchaseRequisitionDetailPanel.jsx lines 180-199 implements modal with correct data-testids (pr-reject-modal, pr-reject-reason, pr-reject-confirm). Confirm button disabled when reason empty, enabled after typing.
h3_pr_reject_modal.backend_api: Backend endpoint /purchase-requisitions/{id}/reject accepts 'notes' field and saves as reject_reason (not hardcoded 'Ditolak via UI').
h3_pr_reject_modal.frontend_test: Could not fully test via Playwright due to no pending PRs available during test run, but implementation verified via code review.

h4_purchase_return_uom.test_result: Created product with base_unit='yard'. Created purchase return with item unit='meter'. Backend correctly derived and stored unit as 'yard' (ignoring client-sent unit).
h4_purchase_return_uom.code_verification: purchase_return_service.py line 72-74: unit = prod.get('base_unit') or (it.unit if it.unit else '') or 'meter'

failed: PR submit by sales (403 - may be intentional)
Supplier update via PUT (405 - API design issue)
